text: This is the assembly process of this reactor.
Step 1, this is the cathode flow plate.
It has a serpentine flow field.
Step 2, this is the cathode electrode.
The cathode is prepared by spray coating copper oxide catalysis onto H23C3 carbon paper.
The catalysis loading is 3 mg per centimeter square.
Step 3, this is the cathode gasket. The gasket thickness is 0.3 micrometer and the inner window size is 1 by 1 centimeter.
Step 4, we place the cathode side membrane.
The cathode side membrane is the alkaline ion exchange membrane.
The cathode side membrane is the alkaline ion exchange membrane.
And this is the middle electrolyte chamber.
The chamber thickness is 3 micrometer and the window size is 1 by 1 centimeter.
The chamber is used to hold and circulate the liquid electrolytes.
The electrolyte is 1 molecule each and it will be continuously circulated through the middle chamber during operation.
During a compare with the previous design, the key change is this reactor is the middle chamber thickness is reduced to 3 micrometer.
The purpose is to shorten the ion transparent distance, reduce the aluminum resistance from the liquid electrolyte layer and improve the overall cell voltage.
In addition, we place a reference electrode in the middle chamber.
The reference electrode is a mercury oxide electrode which allows us to monitor the electrode potentials more accurately during operation.
Step 6, we add the anode gasket. The anode gasket is also 0.3 micrometer.
Step 7, we will replace the anode gasket.
Step 8, we will replace the anode gasket.
Step 9, we will replace the anode gasket.
For the anode side, we also place this alkaline membrane. This membrane is also stainless steel S3750.
Ok, let us change the membrane and gasket position.
